name: Homepride Bank
description_short: HomePride Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank founded in 1892 offering checking, savings, IRAs, and home loans with a focus on personal service.
description_long:
HomePride Bank traces its origins to Bank of Mansfield, established in 1892 with a single location and a commitment to personal service. The institution has grown to include branches in Mansfield, Seymour, Norwood, Fordland, and Ava while maintaining the Coday family's foundational values. The bank emphasizes its long-standing tradition and community presence as core differentiators in the competitive banking landscape.

The bank offers a range of retail and business banking products. For consumers, HomePride provides checking accounts (including NOW Accounts with tiered interest rates), savings products (Kasasa Saver earning 0.50% APY), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and home loan origination. Business customers can access business checking accounts with no fees and free e-banking. The bank also offers remote deposit capabilities, online banking, and financial planning calculators to support customer decision-making.

HomePride distinguishes itself through its community-focused positioning, family ownership continuity, and emphasis on accessible customer service. The bank actively communicates security awareness to customers, publicly warning about phishing attempts and reaffirming that legitimate bank communications will never request personal information via unsolicited calls or clickable links. This transparency around fraud prevention reflects a customer-centric security posture. The availability of in-branch locations allows customers to conduct transactions face-to-face, a feature increasingly rare among banking competitors.

As a regional community bank, HomePride's product suite is more limited than national institutions and online-only banks. The Kasasa Saver rate of 0.50% APY is modest compared to high-yield savings accounts at digital banks. The bank's geographic footprint remains concentrated in rural Missouri, which may limit accessibility for customers outside these specific areas. Prospective customers should verify current product terms and rates directly, as website content does not display specific APY figures for most products or detail loan origination timelines and approval processes. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: FDIC-insured checking accounts (standard and NOW Accounts with tiered interest rates)
Kasasa Saver product with 0.50% APY and no fees
Home loan origination and mortgage services
Business checking accounts with no fees and free e-banking
Bill payment services (implied through check cashing/payment references)
In-branch customer service at five physical locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
132+ years of operational history with continuous family ownership and commitment to service
No fees on business checking accounts with free e-banking included
NOW Accounts feature tiered interest rates that reward higher balances
Kasasa Saver product offers 0.50% APY with no fees and free online banking
Proactive security communication: warns customers about phishing and clarifies legitimate bank contact protocols
Multiple branch locations for in-person service in five Missouri communities
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to rural Missouri (Mansfield, Seymour, Norwood, Fordland, Ava) with no online-only access mentioned
Kasasa Saver rate of 0.50% APY is significantly below high-yield savings accounts offered by national digital banks (typically 4-5% APY)
Website lacks specific APY rates for most products, requiring customers to contact bank directly for accurate rate information
No mention of mobile banking app, mobile deposits, or digital-first features that are standard at competitor banks
Limited product depth compared to large regional or national banks; no credit cards, investment brokerage, or wealth management services mentioned
best_for: Customers in rural Missouri seeking community banking with personal service and branch access
Small business owners in the bank's service area who want no-fee checking with local relationship managers
Conservative savers who prioritize FDIC insurance and long-term institution stability over maximum yields
Homebuyers in Missouri who prefer working with local lenders and community-based loan officers
diagnosis: HomePride Bank is best for customers in rural Missouri communities seeking traditional community banking with personal service, FDIC insurance, and local branch access. The main caveat is that the bank's product yields and digital capabilities lag significantly behind national and online-only competitors, making it most suitable for those who prioritize relationship banking and local presence over maximizing deposit returns or accessing cutting-edge fintech features.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
